subject: ENGLISH
title: Choose an Ending
objective: Choose a logical story ending supported by earlier events.
introduction: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Choose an Ending”.
A key word used in the lesson “Choose an Ending”.
A key word used in the lesson “Choose an Ending”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.

examples: A watered plant stands upright later.
A returned book goes back on its shelf.
A repaired wheel lets the cart move.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

fits: A watered plant stands upright later.
A returned book goes back on its shelf.
A repaired wheel lets the cart move.

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: A seedling is dry and Ana waters it. Which ending fits?

feedback.correct: Correct: The seedling looks healthier later.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. The school becomes a spaceship. and Ana forgets what water is. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

pairs.left: A watered plant stands upright later.
A returned book goes back on its shelf.
A repaired wheel lets the cart move.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Choose an Ending”?

options.text: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

items.explanation: The correct response is “A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

options.text: A watered plant stands upright later.

items.explanation: The correct response is “A watered plant stands upright later.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: A watered plant stands upright later.
A returned book goes back on its shelf.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Adding an unrelated dragon” because it follows the lesson explanation: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

items.prompt: A seedling is dry and Ana waters it. Which ending fits?
items.explanation: The correct response is “The seedling looks healthier later.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: A repaired wheel lets the cart move.

items.explanation: The correct response is “A repaired wheel lets the cart move.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A logical ending follows from the characters, problem, and events already shown.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Choose an Ending; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Choose an Ending.
